TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.TRANSL: We remember you with affection, teacher.

S.FORM: maɫimaɫi aravats a kinuletj sun nua tja Tsemas i vavaw sa su pasamaza i Tayuangi, sa su sikanemanga tua nia kai a katsalisian, sana ne kemɫang anga.
maljimalji aravac a kinuletj sun nua tja Cemas i vavaw sa su pasamaza i Tayuangi, sa su sikanemanga tua nia kai a kacalisian, sana ne kemljang anga.
S.TRANSL: Thank you very much that our God provided you and you were sent here to Taiwan, and you dealt with (?) the language of us people of the mountain slopes and we were able to understand.

S.FORM: pai aitsu a mareka katsalisian nisun a tami.
pai aicu a mareka kacalisian nisun a tami.
S.TRANSL: And so the mountain people are your people.

S.FORM: pai kana ika sun a namangtjez qau kana men a kemɫang tua nia vetsik a kinatsalisianan.
pai kana ika sun a namangtjez qau kana men a kemljang tua nia vecik a kinacalisianan.
S.TRANSL: If you had not come how could (?) we have known the writing for our mountain language?
We are extremely glad about it.

S.FORM: saka pai aitsu nu vaik sun uta uɫa men a su sikainulinuli, uɫa men a temudutudur sakamaya tua nia sinku a mareka su kaka imaza i katsalisian, sana ne kana inulinuli uta tjanusun.
saka pai aicu nu vaik sun uta ulja men a su sikainulinuli, ulja men a temudutudur sakamaya tua nia sinku a mareka su kaka imaza i kacalisian, sana ne kana inulinuli uta tjanusun.
S.TRANSL: And now when you go may you pray for us, so that we your brothers and sisters here in the mountains may continue always in our faith and pray too for you.

S.FORM: nu vaik anga sun nu i djalan anga sun, uɫa sun a napupitsupitsul anga uta; nu i djalan anga sun, uɫa sun a namalevalevanga a mangtjez itua mareka su matjaɫaɫak a mareka su kaka.
nu vaik anga sun nu i djalan anga sun, ulja sun a napupicupicul anga uta; nu i djalan anga sun, ulja sun a namalevalevanga a mangtjez itua mareka su matjaljaljak a mareka su kaka.
S.TRANSL: When you go and are on your journey may you be in good health; and on your way may you be glad to reach your parents and brothers and sisters.

S.FORM: saka pai aitsu a tiamen a uri su vinaikan uɫa men a su kinipaqenetjan sa su kainulinulian amen, uɫa men a napasusu tua tulu nua Tsemas tua kai nua tja kaka ti Yisu-sama, uɫa tjen a namakaya metsevutsevung i tjarivavaw, nu namakapasusu itjen tua tulu nua tja Tsemas i vavaw.

saka pai aicu a tiamen a uri su vinaikan ulja men a su kinipaqenetjan sa su kainulinulian amen, ulja men a napasusu tua tulu nua Cemas tua kai nua tja kaka ti Yisu-sama, ulja tjen a namakaya mecevucevung i tjarivavaw, nu namakapasusu itjen tua tulu nua tja Cemas i vavaw.
S.TRANSL: And we whom you are leaving behind may you remember us and pray for us, so that we will keep the teaching of God and the words of our brother Jesus; may we be able to meet up above if we have fully kept the teaching of our God above.
That’s all I have to say.
